title: C Variable Scope
content:
### Variable Scope in C

Scope determines where a variable is accessible in a program.

### Types of Scope

| Scope | Keyword | Visibility | Lifetime |
|-------|---------|------------|----------|
| Block (local) | (none) | Inside `{ }` block | Until block ends |
| File (global) | (none) | Entire file (after declaration) | Program execution |
| Function | (none) | Inside function only | Function execution |
| Function parameter | (none) | Inside function | Function execution |

### Local Variables

Declared inside a function or block. Accessible only within that block.

```c
void func() {
  int x = 10;  // local to func
  if (x > 0) {
    int y = 20;  // local to if block
  }
  // y is not accessible here
}
```

### Global Variables

Declared outside all functions. Accessible from any function in the file.

```c
int globalVar = 100;  // global

void func1() { globalVar = 200; }
void func2() { printf("%d", globalVar); }
```

### Name Hiding

A local variable with the same name as a global variable hides the global in that scope.

---

> **Key Point:** Prefer local variables over globals. Globals should be used sparingly due to potential naming conflicts and difficulty in debugging.

quiz.question: Where is a local variable declared inside a block accessible?
